rationale: The destabilization in Tethys and weak international response create a narrow window to degrade key military infrastructure with minimal escalation risk.
A forward naval presence off Tethys’ coast amplifies pressure and signals resolve, deterring external intervention during the strike.
Exploit the chaos from the failed coup to test defenses and seize tactically valuable terrain under the guise of “restoring order.”

The political chaos and coup attempt in the defender nation create fleeting opportunities to infiltrate key military and government networks, providing critical insight into Tethys’ stability and response capacity.

Persistent aerial and signals surveillance along the border will detect troop movements and foreign support flows, complementing Krasnov’s limited strike by identifying high-value targets and avoiding unintended escalation.

With sanctions increasing internal economic pressure, the risk of foreign intelligence penetration of our defense sector rises—proactive counterintelligence will protect operational plans and supply chains.

With international support at 70%, we can leverage coalition backing to impose a resource embargo on Tethys, amplifying pressure on its defense sector in line with the recommended strategy and exploiting current diplomatic alignment.

Targeting key individuals and entities within Tethys’ military-industrial complex will complement Krasnov’s limited strike by disrupting command and production nodes without broad humanitarian impact.

Seizing overseas assets of Tethys’ defense leadership will increase internal friction and signal resolve, reinforcing Morozov’s counterintelligence efforts by raising the cost of collaboration under sanctions.
Strengthening our frontline defenses will deny Novaris easy gains and buy time for political and economic stabilization.
Increasing vigilance along contested borders deters opportunistic advances and signals resolve without escalation.
Persistent intelligence gathering on enemy movements is critical to anticipate asymmetric threats and exploit potential overextension.
Given Novaris's shift to asymmetric pressure, we must deepen our understanding of their covert networks and intentions to preempt destabilizing moves.
Continuous monitoring of Novaris military and political activities will provide early warning of further aggression or internal vulnerabilities we can exploit.
Coordinating verified intelligence with Tethys strengthens collective deterrence and reinforces our position within the alliance without requiring kinetic response.

With 70% international support and moderate existing sanctions, we can leverage coalition backing to impose coordinated economic sanctions on Novaris, amplifying pressure on their defense sector without triggering overreach.

Targeting key individuals and entities within Novaris’s military-industrial complex will disrupt procurement networks while maintaining precision to avoid civilian fallout and preserve diplomatic credibility.

Strengthening economic ties with Tethys through trade negotiations will boost our resilience, reward their support, and create interdependence that deepens strategic alignment without requiring military escalation.

Leveraging our 70% international support, we must launch a high-profile diplomatic campaign to frame the airspace violation as a systemic threat to global norms, reinforcing our legal standing and isolating Novaris politically.
By convening a formal forum with Tethys and key neutral states, we institutionalize pressure on Novaris, complementing sanctions and intelligence-sharing with legitimate multilateral scrutiny.

Promoting domestic and international cultural outreach bolsters national unity amid internal instability and projects resilience, countering Novaris’s asymmetric influence without escalating tensions.
